items.prompt: Which sequence best matches the IoT pipeline stages in this part?
choices: Generation, transmission, processing, storage and analytics
HIT posting, then gold standards only
items.explain: Section 9.3.2 walks generation, transmission, processing (edge/cloud), then storage and analytics.

items.prompt: Edge computing processes data at or near the device to reduce cloud load and latency.
items.explain: Edge processing is defined as near-source computation before further cloud analysis.

items.prompt: Example 9.18 (traffic lights) mainly shows that edge processing can:
choices: Replace all cameras with paper surveys
Adjust lights in real time without waiting on a central cloud round trip
Eliminate the need for any sensors
items.explain: Local edge processing enables immediate traffic-light adjustments from cameras and sensors.

items.prompt: Name one edge-computing benefit besides lower latency.
items.rubric: Accept bandwidth optimization, cost reduction, or reliability when the network is temporarily down.
items.sample_answer: Bandwidth optimization: only relevant or pre-processed data is sent to the cloud, reducing bandwidth cost.

items.prompt: Example 9.19 uses real-time truck tracking and traffic data to support logistics rerouting.
items.explain: The logistics example ties continuous tracking to fuel and delivery-time improvements via rerouting.
